summary: High‑quality soundboard recording of a tight 1973 show highlighted by an epic Dark Star and stellar performances from Jerry, Bob and the rhythm section.
review: The tape captures a remarkably tight Dead night with Jerry’s voice in top form, a “tremendous” Dark Star, soaring Wharf Rat and jazzy Eyes of the World.  Listeners note only minor audio hiccups, while the overall SBD sound is clear and well‑balanced, making this a must‑listen for ’73 fans.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently strong musical praise (many 4‑5 star reviews) and mostly excellent soundboard quality; only occasional minor audio issues keep the rating just shy of perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally crisp SBD sound, a few minor mess‑ups noted but no major dropouts or distortion.

poor_songs: Weather Report Suite Parts I & II
show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows tightly with a jazzy first half and an explosive second set; segues (China/Rider, Eyes jams) are praised and the overall pacing keeps the energy high.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocals described as beautiful on Wharf Rat, powerful on They Love Each Other, and generally “great night” for his guitar work.
band_member_comments.Phil: Brief praise after Bertha; otherwise not specifically mentioned.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir called “slashing” and playing very well, providing strong interplay throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Drums: Billy’s drum solo highlighted as a standout moment in the second set.
